title: Wood Star Serving Tray or Wall Decor
summary: A five-pointed star built from compound-angle 1x2 segments backed with plywood, usable flat as a serving tray or hung as wall decor.
description:
Every point of this star is built from two pieces glued at a compound angle, so the two miter saw settings in step one matter more than anything else in the build: first a 30° bevel cutting five 13" boards with parallel ends, then a 24° bevel cutting each of those boards into a shorter and a longer piece. Get those two angles right and the five points close up into a clean star on their own; get one wrong and every joint after it inherits the error.

Build and glue all five points as separate two-piece units first, then join the points together into the full star — trying to align all ten pieces at once invites drift. Trace the assembled star onto the plywood back rather than cutting the backer to a fixed size first, since hand-built compound-angle joints rarely come out at exactly the same size twice.

tools.note: Cutting the plywood backer to the traced star outline.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at a 30° bevel, ends parallel. Each board is further cut in two at a 24° bevel to form the star's 10 point segments.
Traced from the assembled star and cut to fit; 19" x 19" is a starting size.

steps.title: Cut the star point pieces
steps.body: Set the miter saw to a 30° bevel and cut five 1x2s, with ends parallel, to 13" overall length (long point to short point). Then set the miter saw to a 24° bevel and cut each 13" piece into two smaller pieces as shown in the diagram, giving 10 point segments total.

steps.title: Glue each point together
steps.body: Glue one shorter and one longer segment together to form each of the five star points. Hot glue works well, but regular wood glue is fine too — just let it dry. Nailing the joints adds extra strength, though nailing at this angle is a little tricky.

steps.title: Join the points into a star
steps.body: Nail and glue the five point sections together to form the complete star.

steps.title: Cut and attach the backer
steps.body: Place the assembled star on the plywood backer and trace the outline. Cut out the shape with a jigsaw. Glue and nail the backer to the back of the star with shorter nails.

images.alt: A five-pointed wood star serving tray with a plywood backer
